intake.title: Requirement Summary
intake.subtitle: Apex Robotics — Houston Metro

fields.label: Power
fields.value: 480V 3-phase, 1200A minimum. Welding systems draw 800A+ under load.

fields.label: Loading
fields.value: 2 dock-high, 1 grade-level drive-in (12' x 14' for equipment)

dealbreakers: 480V 3-phase 1200A — eliminates most spec buildings under 20K SF
24' clear height — rules out older tilt-wall inventory in NW Houston
Zoning must allow welding and robotic assembly — some parks restrict

comparison.title: Property Comparison
comparison.subtitle: Weighted analysis — 2 properties, 7-year term
comparison.recommendation: Fallbrook wins on functionality (28' clear, 1600A power, crane-ready bay) despite the price premium. The 4' of additional clear height alone justifies the $2.25/SF difference for gantry welding systems.

flags.issue: Lease says 'light assembly and warehousing.' Welding is not light assembly. Landlord could terminate for unauthorized use. Fix: specify 'robotic welding, metal fabrication, and assembly' explicitly.

Lease is silent on transformer capacity. Current building has 1600A but transformer is shared with adjacent tenant. If they expand, your power gets squeezed. Fix: add dedicated transformer clause or minimum guaranteed amperage.

$15/SF TI allowance. Your buildout estimate is $22/SF (compressed air drops, welding ventilation, 3-phase sub-panels). Gap is $127K out of pocket. Negotiate to $20/SF or request landlord-funded electrical upgrade separately.
